complexity.justification: Random-access indexed read-modify-rewrite driven by a hard-coded key/price table, with PERFORM VARYING iteration and error handling on both READ and REWRITE

annotation_metadata.note: Every 'lines' range and 'source_snippet' below was checked line-by-line against the uploaded VSCBEX04.cbl using cat -n; paragraph names are the real COBOL paragraph names.

structures.name: PROGRAM_METADATA
structures.source_snippet:
* Program name:    VSCBEX04
* Original author: dastagg
* Description: Example 04: Indexed File Processing: Update
structures.confidence_justification: Comment header block visually delimited by asterisk banners at lines 1 and 21
structures.description: Program header comment block; states the program reads and updates 5 records in an indexed file opened I-O, ACCESS MODE IS RANDOM

structures.name: IDENTIFICATION
structures.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
CONFIGURATION SECTION.
SOURCE-COMPUTER.   IBM WITH DEBUGGING MODE.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS RANDOM
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / SELECT are explicit. Note SOURCE-COMPUTER...WITH DEBUGGING MODE is NOT commented out here (unlike VSCBEX01-03), so the 'D'-prefixed debug DISPLAY lines in this program are active, not dormant
structures.description: Declares CUSTFile as an indexed customer file opened for random access, keyed on CUSTFile-Cust-ID; debugging mode is active

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.
...
01  WS-Key-HOLD.
    12 FILLER PIC 9(9) VALUE 001000100.
...
18 WS-Key-Value PIC 9(04).
18 WS-Price     PIC 9(3)V99.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit; the packed key+price table is a distinguishing feature versus VSCBEX03/05, which carry only a bare key
structures.description: File record layout, file-status/counters (records read, records re-written), and a hard-coded table of 5 nine-digit values (e.g. 001000100) that each decompose into a 4-digit customer ID plus a 3V2 price (WS-Key-Value / WS-Price) in an OCCURS 5 table

structures.name: MAINLINE
structures.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
structures.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry
structures.description: Program entry point; straight-line perform of begin/process/end phases

structures.name: BEGIN_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    MOVE WS-Key-HOLD  TO WS-Key-Table-Setup.
    OPEN I-O CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Open Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: MOVE and OPEN are direct initialization actions; OPEN I-O (not INPUT) is required because the program both reads and rewrites
structures.description: Loads the 5 hard-coded key/price entries into the working table and opens CUSTFile for random I-O (read and rewrite)

structures.name: PROCESS_LOOP
structures.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    PERFORM VARYING WS-Key-SUB FROM 1 BY 1
       UNTIL WS-Key-SUB > WS-Key-Element-Cnt
       MOVE WS-Key-Value(WS-Key-SUB) TO
          CUSTFile-Cust-ID
       PERFORM 5000-Read-CUSTFile
       PERFORM 2100-Update-CUSTFile
    END-PERFORM.
structures.confidence_justification: PERFORM VARYING...UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit indexed-loop syntax
structures.description: Iterates the 5-entry key table; for each entry sets the record key, reads the matching record, then performs the update/rewrite paragraph

structures.name: UPDATE_RECORD
structures.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE of the table price into the record buffer, followed by an explicit PERFORM of the rewrite paragraph
structures.description: Applies the new price for the current key from the working table into the record buffer, then performs the paragraph that persists the change with REWRITE

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-CUSTFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "Records Re-Written: " FD-CUSTFile-Re-Write-Cnt

    CLOSE CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup
structures.description: Displays end-of-job banner, records-read count, and records-re-written count, then closes CUSTFile

structures.name: READ_RANDOM
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-CUSTFile.
    READ CUSTFile
       RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
    END-READ.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-CUSTFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-CUSTFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: READ...RECORD KEY IS is explicit random-access syntax, but the paragraph combines success counting, a not-found branch, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit
structures.description: Performs a keyed random read against CUSTFile using the current key; increments the read counter on success, and on a genuine I/O error logs diagnostics and abends

structures.name: REWRITE_CUSTFILE
structures.source_snippet:
6000-Re-Write-CUSTFile.
    REWRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Re-Write-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Re-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Re-Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: REWRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE (no INVALID KEY clause is used)
structures.description: Rewrites the just-read, now-modified record back to CUSTFile at its existing key position; increments the re-write counter on success, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8
